title: haplopappus velutinus subsp. velutinus
intro: Haplopappus velutinus subsp. velutinus, also known as Woolly Haplopappus, is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family, native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids. The plant has been found to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and exhibiting antimicrobial activity, making it a potential treatment for various health conditions. Herbal preparations of Woolly Haplopappus include tea, tincture, and infusion, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and reap its health benefits.
